summary: Matched dolphin/card CARDBios by replacing the 1383-line assembly file with a 618-line C implementation, wiring it into obj_files.mk and the build's Frank post-processing path for src/dolphin/card objects. The PR also expanded dolphin/card.h with CARD result codes, CARDFileInfo/CARDDir/CARDControl layouts, callbacks, and internal prototypes, then updated remaining CARD asm files to reference the new C globals __CARDBlock and __CARDDiskNone instead of raw lbl_804A8B38-style labels. Supporting header/API fixes moved OSResetFunctionInfo to OSReset.h, added OSSetAlarm declaration, typed DVD ResetAlarm as OSAlarm, and exposed __CARDSetDiskID through dvd.h.

key_files.role: Deleted matched assembly source for CARDBios.
New matched C implementation containing __CARDBlock, __CARDDiskNone, callbacks, EXI transfer helpers, CARDInit, control-block helpers, sync, and reset handling.
Expanded CARD API/constants and introduced CARDFileInfo, CARDDir, and CARDControl layouts needed by CARDBios and existing CARD asm.
Swapped CARDBios from asm object to C object.
Added Frank rule for src/dolphin/card/%.c.o objects.
Updated dependent CARD assembly files to reference named C globals such as __CARDBlock and __CARDDiskNone.
Moved OSResetFunction and OSResetFunctionInfo declarations into the public OSReset header.
Removed local OSResetFunctionInfo typedef now provided by the header.
Declared OSSetAlarm for CARDBios/DVD alarm use.
Retyped ResetAlarm as OSAlarm and updated OSSetAlarm usage/signature.
Adjusted DVD callback parameter typing to DVDCommandBlock*.

what_changed: Removed asm/dolphin/card/CARDBios.s and added src/dolphin/card/CARDBios.c as the matched source for CARDBios.
Implemented CARDBios globals CARDControl __CARDBlock[2] and DVDDiskID __CARDDiskNone, allowing other files to reference meaningful symbols instead of anonymous labels.

Implemented CARD internal handlers and helpers visible in the diff: __CARDDefaultApiCallback, __CARDSyncCallback, __CARDExtHandler, __CARDExiHandler, __CARDTxHandler, __CARDUnlockedHandler, __CARDEnableInterrupt, __CARDReadStatus, __CARDClearStatus, __CARDStart, __CARDReadSegment, __CARDWritePage, __CARDEraseSector, CARDInit, __CARDSetDiskID, __CARDGetControlBlock, __CARDPutControlBlock, CARDGetResultCode, CARDFreeBlocks, CARDGetSectorSize, __CARDSync, and reset callback OnReset.

Added CARD constants for result codes and structural constants such as CARD_RESULT_NOCARD, CARD_RESULT_IOERROR, CARD_RESULT_FATAL_ERROR, CARD_NUM_SYSTEM_BLOCK, CARD_FILENAME_MAX, CARD_MAX_FILE, and CARD_MAX_MOUNT_STEP.
Defined CARDCallback plus CARDFileInfo, CARDDir, and CARDControl in include/dolphin/card.h, including callback fields, thread queue, command buffer, retry state, OSAlarm, and DVDDiskID pointer.
Changed dependent CARD assembly to load __CARDBlock@ha/__CARDBlock@l and __CARDDiskNone@ha/__CARDDiskNone@l instead of lbl_804A8B38/raw addresses.
Added a Makefile Frank rule for src/dolphin/card/%.c.o and switched obj_files.mk from asm/dolphin/card/CARDBios.s.o to src/dolphin/card/CARDBios.c.o.
Added OSResetFunctionInfo declarations to OSReset.h and removed the duplicate local declarations from OSReset.c.
Declared OSSetAlarm in OSAlarm.h and updated dvd.c to use OSAlarm ResetAlarm, OSCreateAlarm(&ResetAlarm), and OSSetAlarm(&ResetAlarm, OSMillisecondsToTicks(1150), AlarmHandler).
Exposed __CARDSetDiskID(const DVDDiskID* id) in dvd.h and had CARDInit set disk IDs from 0x80000000 while __CARDSetDiskID falls back to __CARDDiskNone for null IDs.
smart_moves: Converted the main CARDBios unit first while preserving dependent CARD modules as assembly, then repaired their symbol references to the new C globals. This is a practical incremental decomp strategy for tightly coupled libraries.
Put the large CARDControl layout in the shared header so existing asm and future C ports can converge on one offset model instead of repeating raw lbl/offset knowledge.
Used named result macros instead of magic negative values in the new C, making EXI/card error flow easier to audit while still matching the underlying constants.
Moved OSResetFunctionInfo into OSReset.h rather than duplicating it in OSReset.c, enabling CARDBios reset registration without private typedef drift.
Typed ResetAlarm and callback signatures in DVD code to match the OSAlarm API, which reduced raw byte-buffer use and supported the new OSSetAlarm prototype.
Added a card-specific Frank object rule, acknowledging that newly decompiled dolphin/card C needed the same post-processing support already used for other source areas.
decomp_lessons: When a C file introduces a global previously represented by an anonymous data label, update all remaining assembly users immediately to the new symbol name. In this PR, many CARD asm files changed lbl_804A8B38 references to __CARDBlock.
Large library matches often require header work outside the target file: CARDBios needed CARD structs, OSAlarm declarations, OSReset registration structs, DVD disk ID types, and build glue.

Preserving binary behavior for asynchronous hardware code means modeling callbacks, alarms, locks, and retry paths explicitly; CARDBios centers on EXILock/EXISelect/EXIImm/EXIDma/EXIDeselect/EXIUnlock plus OSAlarm timeout handling.

Typed structs can unlock matching progress in adjacent assembly because field offsets become named members such as CARDControl.threadQueue, alarm, cmd, mode, retry, diskID, callbacks, currentDir, and currentFat.
For old SDK-style code, matching may involve SDK idioms such as global reset-function registration, OS bus/timer clock conversions, and device command words rather than only local control flow.
Diff evidence contains no PR body or review comments explaining rationale, so lessons are inferred from file changes and code patterns rather than explicit author/reviewer discussion.
naming_conventions: Internal CARD symbols use the double-underscore SDK style: __CARDBlock, __CARDDiskNone, __CARDStart, __CARDReadStatus, __CARDClearStatus, __CARDGetControlBlock, __CARDPutControlBlock, __CARDSync.
Public CARD APIs use CARD-prefixed names without the internal double underscore: CARDInit, CARDGetResultCode, CARDFreeBlocks, CARDGetSectorSize.

Result constants use CARD_RESULT_* names and preserve SDK numeric semantics, including CARD_RESULT_UNLOCKED = 1, CARD_RESULT_READY = 0, CARD_RESULT_BUSY = -1, CARD_RESULT_NOCARD = -3, CARD_RESULT_IOERROR = -5, and CARD_RESULT_FATAL_ERROR = -128.
CARD struct names follow SDK-style nouns: CARDFileInfo, CARDDir, CARDControl, CARDCallback.
OS reset callback metadata uses OSResetFunction and OSResetFunctionInfo in include/dolphin/os/OSReset.h.
Disk ID fallback global is named __CARDDiskNone and is referenced by remaining CARDOpen assembly.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the assembly object in obj_files.mk with the new C object: asm/dolphin/card/CARDBios.s.o became src/dolphin/card/CARDBios.c.o.
Added Makefile support for Frank to post-process src/dolphin/card/%.c.o, matching the pattern already used for other source directories.
Kept dependent CARD modules in assembly but patched their relocations to named symbols, especially __CARDBlock and __CARDDiskNone, preserving linkability during incremental decomp.

Modeled command-word setup directly in C for EXI transactions, e.g. 0x81010000/0x81000000 for interrupt enable, 0x83000000 for read status, 0x89000000 for clear status, 0x52 for read segment, 0xF2 for write page, and 0xF1 for erase sector.
Used small address macros AD1, AD2, AD3, and BA to reproduce memory-card command-byte construction from card addresses.
Represented timeout setup with OSSetAlarm and tick conversion macros based on __OSBusClock, matching low-level timing behavior needed by card writes/erases.

review_feedback.note: No issue comments, review comments, or review body text were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile CARDUnlock, CARDRdrw, CARDBlock, CARDMount, CARDOpen, CARDRead, or CARDWrite after __CARDBlock was introduced.
Search for changes to CARDControl layout fields to see whether offsets from PR 615 were corrected later.
Search for later cleanup of OSAlarm/OSSetAlarm prototypes and DVD ResetAlarm typing.
Search for __CARDDiskNone and __CARDSetDiskID usages in later DVD/CARD integration work.
Compare later CARDBios.c revisions for renamed Retry, timeout macros, or static/internal prototype cleanup.
